prompt:
Frontend request. Our checkout has three hand-rolled masked inputs (phone, license plate, gift codes) and each one has its own cursor-jumping bugs, so I want the masking logic pulled out into a pure, framework-free module we can unit test: mask.ts, exporting applyMask(raw, mask, cursor?) and unmask(masked, mask). No DOM, no events — the React wrapper stays ours, this module just does strings.

Mask language: '#' and '9' are both digit slots, 'A' is a letter slot (store it uppercased), '*' takes a letter or a digit and keeps whatever case was typed. Anything else is a literal that the engine inserts by itself. A backslash escapes the next character into a literal so 'v\\9-99' is a literal v, a literal 9, a dash, then two digit slots.

applyMask returns { masked, complete, cursor }. It applies the raw characters progressively: literals appear once the user reaches them ('4155' against '(###) ###-####' is '(415) 5', with nothing padded out beyond what was typed), a raw character that matches the upcoming literal is swallowed (pasting an already-formatted '(415) 555-1234' must be a no-op), characters that fit neither the current slot nor the upcoming literal are rejected outright, and typing past the last slot does nothing. complete is true when every slot is filled.

The cursor argument is an index into raw; the returned cursor is the matching index in masked — after the masked image of everything accepted from raw before that index. Rejected characters must not move it, auto-inserted literals must. If no cursor is passed, return the position at the end of the masked text. This is the piece our current inputs get wrong.

unmask walks the mask against a masked string and hands back just the slot characters, working on partial values too, and unmask(applyMask(x).masked) has to round-trip. It should throw — with the offending character or expected literal in the message — when a literal doesn't line up, a slot holds the wrong class of character, or the text runs past the mask.

Acceptance tests: test_mask.ts, `node --test test_mask.ts`. Test file is off limits. No dependencies.
